name: 11th Avenue Hostel
description: Denver's oldest hostel, built in 1903, fully renovated 2022. Dorms and private rooms, on-site café, communal kitchen, guided city tours, bar crawls. 24/7 staff.

name: Hostel Fish
description: Upscale boutique hostel in the historic Airedale Building, 2 blocks from Coors Field. Dorms and private suites, nightclub/bar, terrace, arcade/game room. 'Of backpackers, by backpackers.'

name: Ember Hostel
description: 9,000 sq ft historic Capitol Hill mansion. Co-ed and female-only dorms, outdoor hot tub, firepit, regular events including Red Rocks excursions, yoga, movie nights. Rated 3rd best hostel in USA by Hostelworld (2025). Adults 18+ only.

name: A-Lodge Boulder
description: 7-acre adventure lodge on Fourmile Creek, 2 miles from Pearl St. Hostel bunks, hotel rooms, tent/vehicle camping, 3BR cabin. Seasonal pool, year-round hot tub, saloon, on-site trails, slackline park.

name: A-Lodge Lyons
description: Adventure lodging in downtown Lyons, next to Oskar Blues Brewery. Hotel rooms, 2BR suite, and bunk family rooms. Gateway to St. Vrain Canyon climbing and RMNP south approach.

name: Estes Park Adventure Hostel
description: Walking distance to historic downtown Estes Park and Aerial Tramway, 5 min from RMNP entrance. Host greets guests on arrival. Adults 18+ only.

name: Shadowcliff Mountain Lodge & Retreat Center
description: Nonprofit seasonal lodge (late May–late September) on western edge of Rocky Mountain National Park. Hostel bunk sections (men's/women's), lodge rooms, 3 cabins. Hand-built by volunteers since 1959. Closed Oct–May.
hours: Seasonal: late May through late September only

name: The Bivvi Hostel — Breckenridge
description: One of Colorado's best-known ski-town hostels. Indoor hot tub, free breakfast, bar, ski/snowboard/bike storage, dorms and private rooms. Walking distance to downtown Breckenridge. Books fast in ski season.

name: Fireside Inn & Hostel
description: B&B and hostel hybrid in downtown Breckenridge. Dorm beds with shared bathrooms alongside B&B suites with private baths. Owner-operated, warm community feel.

name: Bunk House Lodge
description: LGBTQ+-welcoming hostel 2 miles north of downtown Breckenridge. Custom pod-style bunk beds, continental breakfast, bar, board games. Adults 21+ only. 420-friendly.

name: Treeline Hostel
description: Highest-elevation hostel in North America at 10,578 ft. 4 private rooms + 2 bunk rooms, capacity 24. Large communal kitchen, ski/bike storage, free parking. 16 miles south of Breckenridge (free ski bus).

name: The Block Hotel & Commons
description: Formerly 'The Pad', Silverthorne's boutique ski hostel. Bunk dorms, private rooms, rooftop hot tub, bar, communal kitchen. River views, free ski bus to Breckenridge/Copper/A-Basin/Loveland. Daily events: trivia, yoga, dance.

name: The Bunkhouse (Vail Valley)
description: 38-bed boutique hostel in Minturn, 12 min from Vail — the only boutique hostel in the Vail Valley. Custom sound-isolated pod bunks with USB/power outlets. 24-hr communal kitchen, free coffee, beer/wine service, free summer concerts.

name: Glenwood Springs Hostel
description: Long-established hostel in downtown Glenwood Springs. NOTE: Property appeared for sale in 2026 — verify operating status before booking.

name: St. Moritz Lodge (Hostel Rooms)
description: Aspen's only bunk option. 13 'private hostel' rooms with shared bathrooms. All lodge amenities: free breakfast, heated pool, steam room, free ski shuttle. Budget end of a full lodge — not a pure backpacker hostel.

name: Simple Lodge & Hostel
description: Owner-operated, hosts live on-site. Downtown Salida, 1 block from the Arkansas River. Bunk beds ~$22–25/night, private rooms, communal kitchen, free bikes. Capacity ~20+.

name: The Salida Inn & Hostel
description: Small inn and hostel with private access to the Arkansas River. Private rooms and a bunkroom. Friendly, social atmosphere. Minutes from downtown Salida.

name: Inn the Clouds Hostel & Inn
description: Claims 'highest hostel in the USA' — Leadville is the highest incorporated US city. Male-only and female-only dorm sections plus private rooms. Full communal kitchen, gear storage (skis/bikes/packs), pet-friendly.

name: Colorado Trail Hostel
description: Victorian home bunkhouse designed on European hostel model, ~$25/night. Stocked communal kitchen, shared living spaces. Serves Colorado Trail and 14er hikers. Call ahead to confirm current operations.

name: The Wanderlust Hostel
description: Year-round hostel near downtown Gunnison, 28 miles from Crested Butte. Free bikes, communal kitchen, game room, garden, BBQ grills, laundry. Bunk rates from $25–57/night. Pet-friendly for a fee.

name: RMBL 365 (formerly Crested Butte Hostel)
description: Sold to Rocky Mountain Biological Laboratory (RMBL) in October 2025. Operating as RMBL 365 science hub while continuing public hostel beds and the town's only commercial laundry. Verify booking under new nonprofit management.

name: Raven's Rest Hostel
description: Budget bunk hostel (~$25/night) in Lake City, key Colorado Trail and CDT resupply stop. WARNING: Reddit posts from June 2024 report website down, possible closure. Verify before traveling.

name: The Bivvi Hostel — Telluride (Placerville)
description: 14-bedroom boutique hostel/lodge near Telluride along the San Miguel River. Bar, free continental breakfast, hot tubs, pool table, ski/board storage, free parking. Fly fishing on-site.

name: Mine Shaft Inn
description: Historic Victorian hotel and hostel, 30 min from Telluride in Rico, surrounded by San Juan National Forest. Full kitchen access for all guests, free breakfast buffet, library, fireplace lounge. Community-oriented for hikers, skiers, cyclists.

name: Dolores Bike Hostel
description: Boutique adventure hostel in downtown Dolores (Four Corners area). Bunk rooms, private rooms, 5-person family suite. Sauna and cold plunge, bar, yoga, book club. Near Mesa Verde NP. All travelers welcome.

name: The Junction Hotel & Hostel
description: Hotel/motel complex with dedicated hostel rooms, shared kitchen, communal lounge, pool access. Near Purgatory Resort, mountain biking, Animas River. Adults 21+ only.

name: The Avon Hotel & Hostel
description: Historic hotel built 1904, renovated 2017–18. 14 guestrooms, live music venue, bar and café. Budget/hostel-style rooms alongside standard hotel accommodations. San Juan Mountain adventure base.

name: Kendall Mountain Lodge (fka Blair Street Hostel)
description: 9-room property on historic Blair Street in Silverton. Ski-in/out, BBQ/picnic area, 24-hr reception. Previously listed as Blair Street Hostel on some booking platforms.

name: Divide Rider's Hostel
description: Remote cabin hostel for 10 guests, 16 miles from Wolf Creek Ski Area. Rustic, art-filled, well-equipped kitchen. WARNING: No recent web presence found as of 2026 (last confirmed active ~2019). Call/email before traveling.

name: The Local Nomad
description: Downtown Paonia hostel with private rooms (no dorm bunks), shared bathrooms, fully equipped communal kitchen, art room, yard, balcony hammocks. Walkable to shops, galleries, live music. Rates $60–120/night.

name: Two Bridges Lodge
description: Riverside resort that opens seasonal hostel-style beds for Colorado Trail hikers/cyclists each summer. Sauna, steam room, cold plunge, private fly fishing. 1 hour from Denver. Seasonal only — confirm availability.

name: RAD Hostel
description: Award-winning community hostel with mission 'to cure loneliness.' 6 dorm rooms (up to 12-person), 8 private rooms, car camping. Daily events: bonfires, community dinners, group hikes to Garden of the Gods, live music, game nights. Free breakfast, bikes, laundry.
address: 3844 N Nevada Ave, Colorado Springs, CO 80907
